prompt:
You are an AI ATC decision-support agent.

Current time: 2026-03-15 14:00:00 UTC

Two aircraft are in en-route Class A airspace. You have access to separation_calculator and time_window_checker tools.

Aircraft 1 — AAL301 (B737, Large): Position 40.000°N 080.000°W, FL350, Heading 090 (eastbound), Speed 450 kts
Aircraft 2 — DAL402 (A320, Large): Position 40.000°N 079.600°W, FL350, Heading 270 (westbound), Speed 420 kts

Trajectory (SYNTHETIC): Both aircraft flying straight-line constant-altitude tracks at constant speed.

A conflict alert advisory window is active from 2026-03-15 13:55:00 UTC to 2026-03-15 14:10:00 UTC.

Step 1: Call separation_calculator to determine current horizontal separation.
Step 2: Call time_window_checker to confirm the conflict advisory is currently active.
Step 3: Calculate time to 5 NM separation threshold.
Step 4: Recommend an ATC resolution action.

Provide tool call arguments and final recommendation.
gold_decision:
TOOL CALLS AND ANALYSIS:

Step 1 — separation_calculator:
Args: lat1_deg=40.000, lon1_deg=-80.000, lat2_deg=40.000, lon2_deg=-79.600
Expected result: distance_nm ≈ 20.47 NM (haversine at 40°N: 0.400° × 60 × cos(40°) = 0.400 × 60 × 0.7660 = 18.38 NM). Note: actual haversine value.
Actual calculation: at 40.000°N, cos(40°)=0.7660; lon diff = 0.400°; distance = 0.400 × 60 × 0.7660 = 18.38 NM.

Step 2 — time_window_checker:
Args: query_time=2026-03-15T14:00:00+00:00, window_start=2026-03-15T13:55:00+00:00, window_end=2026-03-15T14:10:00+00:00
Expected result: status=ACTIVE, seconds_until_end=600

Step 3 — Time to 5 NM threshold:
Closing rate: 450 + 420 = 870 kts (reciprocal headings).
Time to 5 NM: (18.38 - 5.0) / 870 × 60 = 13.38/870 × 60 = 0.01538 hr × 60 = 0.9231 min ≈ 55 seconds.

Step 4 — RECOMMENDATION: IMMEDIATE ACTION REQUIRED. The aircraft will reach the 5 NM separation minimum in approximately 55 seconds. Both are at FL350 with zero vertical separation. Recommended action: IMMEDIATELY assign DAL402 to descend to FL340 or AAL301 to climb to FL360 to establish 1,000 ft vertical separation. Alternatively: vector one aircraft 30° off-track. Do not delay — 55-second window is insufficient for speed-only solutions.
required_safety_constraints: Time to 5 NM is approximately 55 seconds — immediate action required, no time for deliberation
Vertical separation (1,000 ft) is the fastest resolution in RVSM airspace — one altitude change
Conflict advisory window confirmed ACTIVE per time_window_checker — advisory is current
acceptable_variants: Haversine result may differ slightly from simple cos approximation — accept range 18.3-18.5 NM; time to 5 NM range 50-60 seconds
evidence_requirements: separation_calculator tool output (haversine distance)
time_window_checker tool output (advisory window active)
FAA Order JO 7110.65Z §5-5-4 — 5 NM en-route minimum
FAA Order JO 7110.65Z §4-6-1 — 1,000 ft RVSM vertical as resolution

provenance.generation_rule: Two reciprocal-track aircraft at FL350. AAL301 at 40.000°N 080.000°W (450 kt eastbound), DAL402 at 40.000°N 079.600°W (420 kt westbound). Lon diff 0.400° at 40°N: 0.400 × 60 × cos(40°) = 0.400 × 60 × 0.7660 = 18.38 NM. Closing rate 870 kt. Time to 5 NM: (18.38-5)/870 × 60 = 55 sec. Type D requires: separation_calculator(40.0, -80.0, 40.0, -79.6) and time_window_checker(query=14:00Z, start=13:55Z, end=14:10Z). Conflict advisory window active.

prompt:
You are an AI ATC decision-support agent.

Current time: 2026-05-10 09:30:00 UTC

Three aircraft in en-route RVSM airspace. Use separation_calculator and time_window_checker.

Aircraft 1 — N501 (B777, Heavy): Position 35.000°N 105.000°W, FL370, Heading 090, Speed 490 kts
Aircraft 2 — N602 (B737, Large): Position 35.000°N 104.870°W, FL370, Heading 090, Speed 460 kts
Aircraft 3 — N703 (A320, Large): Position 35.100°N 104.870°W, FL390, Heading 090, Speed 455 kts

A traffic management restriction window for the eastern sector is active: 2026-05-10 09:00:00 UTC to 2026-05-10 10:00:00 UTC.

Trajectory (SYNTHETIC): All three aircraft flying straight-line eastbound constant-altitude tracks.

Step 1: Check N501-N602 horizontal separation.
Step 2: Check N602-N703 horizontal AND vertical separation.
Step 3: Check if the traffic restriction window is currently active.
Step 4: Identify any violations and recommend resolution.
gold_decision:
TOOL CALLS:

Step 1 — separation_calculator(N501 vs N602):
Args: lat1_deg=35.000, lon1_deg=-105.000, lat2_deg=35.000, lon2_deg=-104.870
Lon diff = 0.130°. At 35°N, cos(35°) = 0.8192. Distance = 0.130 × 60 × 0.8192 = 6.39 NM.
6.39 NM > 5 NM: N501-N602 horizontal separation is COMPLIANT. Closing rate: 490 - 460 = 30 kts (N501 faster, catching N602). Time to 5 NM: (6.39 - 5.0)/30 × 60 = 1.39/30 × 60 = 2.78 minutes. Will reach minimum in 2.78 min — WATCH closely.

Step 2 — separation_calculator(N602 vs N703):
Args: lat1_deg=35.000, lon1_deg=-104.870, lat2_deg=35.100, lon2_deg=-104.870
Lat diff only: 0.100° = 6.0 NM horizontal.
Vertical: FL390 - FL370 = 2,000 ft. Both in RVSM — 1,000 ft minimum. 2,000 ft > 1,000 ft: COMPLIANT.

Step 3 — time_window_checker:
Args: query_time=2026-05-10T09:30:00+00:00, window_start=2026-05-10T09:00:00+00:00, window_end=2026-05-10T10:00:00+00:00
Result: ACTIVE, 1,800 seconds remaining.

Step 4 — FINDINGS:
1. N501-N602: Currently 6.39 NM compliant but closing at 30 kts — will reach 5 NM minimum in 2.78 minutes. ALERT: Speed control required. Assign N501 to Mach reduction or vector. Both at FL370 = zero vertical.
2. N602-N703: 6.0 NM lateral + 2,000 ft vertical = double-layer separation, compliant.
3. Traffic restriction ACTIVE — no additional departures should enter the restricted sector during this window.
RECOMMENDATION: Reduce N501 speed by 30+ kts (or vector 10° off-track) to stop the N501-N602 closing. Issue advisory to N602 crew.
required_safety_constraints: N501 closing on N602 at 30 kts — will reach 5 NM minimum in 2.78 minutes without intervention
N602-N703 vertical 2,000 ft is compliant and safe
Traffic restriction window confirmed active — sector entry must be controlled
acceptable_variants: N501-N602: haversine 0.130° at 35°N: 0.130 × 60 × 0.8192 = 6.39 NM; accept ±0.1 NM; time to 5 NM: 2.78 min ±0.2 min
evidence_requirements: separation_calculator(35.0, -105.0, 35.0, -104.87) for N501-N602
separation_calculator(35.0, -104.87, 35.1, -104.87) for N602-N703 horizontal
time_window_checker(09:30Z, 09:00Z, 10:00Z)
FAA Order JO 7110.65Z §5-5-4 — 5 NM en-route minimum

provenance.generation_rule: Three eastbound aircraft at 35°N. N501 (490 kt) at 105°W, N602 (460 kt) at 104.870°W (0.130° = 6.39 NM gap), N703 (455 kt) at 35.100°N 104.870°W FL390. N501 closing on N602 at 30 kt; time to 5 NM = 2.78 min. N602-N703: 6 NM lateral + 2000 ft vertical. Traffic window 09:00-10:00Z active at 09:30Z. Type D requires three tool calls: two separation_calculator and one time_window_checker.

prompt:
You are an AI ATC decision-support agent.

Current time: 2026-07-20 16:45:00 UTC

Terminal approach environment. Use separation_calculator.

Aircraft 1 — UAL800 (B757, Large-special): Position 33.942°N 118.415°W, 5,000 ft, Heading 250, Speed 180 kts (ILS 25L inbound)
Aircraft 2 — ASA901 (A320, Large): Position 33.936°N 118.360°W, 5,000 ft, Heading 250, Speed 175 kts (ILS 25L inbound, same runway, behind UAL800)

Trajectory (SYNTHETIC): Both aircraft on straight-line ILS 25L final approach, constant altitude.

Note: B757 has special wake turbulence provisions — treat as equivalent to Heavy for following aircraft separation.

Step 1: Calculate current horizontal separation between UAL800 and ASA901.
Step 2: Determine the required wake turbulence separation minimum (B757 leading, A320 following).
Step 3: Is current separation compliant? If not, what action is required?
gold_decision:
TOOL CALLS:

Step 1 — separation_calculator:
Args: lat1_deg=33.942, lon1_deg=-118.415, lat2_deg=33.936, lon2_deg=-118.360
Lat diff: 33.942-33.936 = 0.006°; lon diff: 118.415-118.360 = 0.055°
At 33.939°N avg, cos(33.939°) ≈ 0.8302.
Lat component: 0.006 × 60 = 0.36 NM
Lon component: 0.055 × 60 × 0.8302 = 2.74 NM
Total: √(0.36² + 2.74²) = √(0.130 + 7.508) = √7.638 = 2.764 NM ≈ 2.76 NM

Step 2 — Wake turbulence minimum:
B757 has special wake provisions: per FAA Order JO 7110.65Z §7-4-3g, B757 as leader requires Heavy-like separation. A320 (Large) following B757 = same as Large following Heavy = 5 NM minimum.

Step 3 — COMPLIANCE: 2.76 NM current separation vs 5.0 NM required. ACTIVE WAKE TURBULENCE SEPARATION VIOLATION.
2.76 NM is well below the 5 NM B757-special minimum.
RECOMMENDATION: Issue IMMEDIATE go-around to ASA901 or extend ASA901 downwind to achieve 5 NM separation. ASA901 must not continue the approach at current spacing behind a B757.
required_safety_constraints: B757 special wake turbulence provision requires Heavy-like following separation: 5 NM for Large following B757 per FAA Order JO 7110.65Z §7-4-3g
2.76 NM << 5 NM — critical wake turbulence separation violation on final approach
Immediate go-around or extended downwind required — speed reduction alone cannot create 2.24 NM of additional spacing on final approach
acceptable_variants: Haversine result: lat diff 0.006° = 0.36 NM, lon diff 0.055° at 33.939°N = 2.74 NM, total ≈ 2.76 NM; accept ±0.1 NM
evidence_requirements: FAA Order JO 7110.65Z §7-4-3g — B757 special wake turbulence provisions
FAA Order JO 7110.65Z Table 5-5-4 — Large following Heavy = 5 NM on final approach

provenance.generation_rule: B757 (special wake) at 33.942°N 118.415°W, A320 (Large) at 33.936°N 118.360°W on ILS 25L. Lat diff 0.006° = 0.36 NM, lon diff 0.055° at 33.939°N = 2.74 NM, total 2.76 NM. Required: Large following B757-special = 5 NM (same as Heavy per JO 7110.65Z §7-4-3g). Violation: 2.76 < 5 NM. Type D requires separation_calculator call and knowledge of B757 special rule.

prompt:
You are an AI ATC decision-support agent.

Current time: 2026-01-08 11:00:00 UTC

En-route airspace, RVSM band (FL290-FL410). Use separation_calculator and time_window_checker.

Aircraft 1 — LH401 (B747-8, Heavy): Position 51.000°N 015.000°W, FL380, Heading 270 (westbound), Speed 500 kts
Aircraft 2 — BA202 (B787, Heavy): Position 51.000°N 015.600°W, FL380, Heading 270 (westbound), Speed 480 kts

LH401 is BEHIND BA202 on the same westbound track. LH401 faster.

An RVSM monitoring window is active 2026-01-08 10:30:00 UTC to 2026-01-08 12:00:00 UTC.

Trajectory (SYNTHETIC): Both aircraft flying straight-line westbound constant-altitude tracks.

Step 1: Calculate horizontal separation.
Step 2: Calculate time before LH401 will reach the 5 NM minimum.
Step 3: Verify RVSM monitoring window is active.
Step 4: Recommend resolution.
gold_decision:
TOOL CALLS:

Step 1 — separation_calculator:
Args: lat1_deg=51.000, lon1_deg=-15.000, lat2_deg=51.000, lon2_deg=-15.600
Note: LH401 is at -15.000°W (further east = behind westbound traffic), BA202 is at -15.600°W (further west = ahead).
Lon diff: 15.600 - 15.000 = 0.600°. At 51°N, cos(51°) = 0.6293. Distance = 0.600 × 60 × 0.6293 = 22.65 NM.

Step 2 — Time to 5 NM:
Closing rate: 500 - 480 = 20 kts (LH401 catching BA202).
Time to 5 NM: (22.65 - 5.0)/20 × 60 = 17.65/20 × 60 = 52.95 minutes.

Step 3 — time_window_checker:
Args: query_time=2026-01-08T11:00:00+00:00, window_start=2026-01-08T10:30:00+00:00, window_end=2026-01-08T12:00:00+00:00
Result: ACTIVE, 3,600 seconds remaining.

Step 4 — ANALYSIS:
Current separation 22.65 NM is well above 5 NM minimum. Closing at 20 kts, the 5 NM minimum will be reached in approximately 53 minutes. Both aircraft at FL380 — zero vertical separation in RVSM band.
RECOMMENDATION: No immediate action required. Issue speed reduction to LH401 (reduce by 20+ kts) within the next 20-30 minutes to prevent future violation. Alternatively, issue altitude change (FL390 or FL370) to one aircraft for 1,000 ft vertical separation. RVSM monitoring active — track altimetry performance of both aircraft. Log this pair for conflict monitoring.
required_safety_constraints: 22.65 NM current separation compliant; 53 minutes to minimum threshold — proactive action within 20-30 min
RVSM monitoring window active — both aircraft altimetry must meet ±65 ft accuracy standard
Zero vertical at FL380: only horizontal protection — speed control or altitude change required before 5 NM threshold
evidence_requirements: separation_calculator(51.0, -15.0, 51.0, -15.6)
time_window_checker(11:00Z, 10:30Z, 12:00Z)
FAA Order JO 7110.65Z §5-5-4 — 5 NM en-route minimum
FAA Order JO 7110.65Z §4-6-1 — RVSM 1,000 ft minimum

provenance.generation_rule: Two westbound heavy aircraft at FL380 at 51°N. LH401 at 015.000°W (behind), BA202 at 015.600°W (ahead). Lon diff 0.600° at 51°N: 0.600 × 60 × cos(51°) = 22.65 NM. Closing 20 kt. Time to 5 NM: 52.95 min. RVSM monitoring window 10:30-12:00Z active at 11:00Z. No immediate violation; proactive action needed within 30 min. Type D: separation_calculator + time_window_checker + analysis.

prompt:
You are an AI ATC decision-support agent.

Current time: 2026-04-22 20:15:00 UTC

En-route airspace. Use separation_calculator and time_window_checker.

Aircraft 1 — WN1001 (B737, Large): Position 32.000°N 096.500°W, FL330, Heading 000 (northbound), Speed 420 kts
Aircraft 2 — AA2002 (A321, Large): Position 33.000°N 096.500°W, FL330, Heading 180 (southbound), Speed 430 kts

Both on same longitude, converging head-on at FL330 with zero vertical separation.

A conflict alert window is active: 2026-04-22 20:10:00 UTC to 2026-04-22 20:25:00 UTC.

Trajectory (SYNTHETIC): Both aircraft flying straight-line constant-altitude tracks on the same longitude.

Step 1: Calculate current horizontal separation.
Step 2: Determine time to 5 NM separation.
Step 3: Confirm conflict alert window active.
Step 4: Recommend ATC resolution with tool-based justification.
gold_decision:
TOOL CALLS:

Step 1 — separation_calculator:
Args: lat1_deg=32.000, lon1_deg=-96.500, lat2_deg=33.000, lon2_deg=-96.500
Lat diff: 33.000 - 32.000 = 1.000°. Both on same longitude. Distance = 1.000 × 60 = 60.0 NM (latitude degrees are constant = 60 NM per degree).

Step 2 — Time to 5 NM:
Closing rate: 420 + 430 = 850 kts (head-on, same longitude, opposite headings).
Time to 5 NM: (60.0 - 5.0)/850 × 60 = 55.0/850 × 60 = 3.882 min ≈ 3.88 minutes.

Step 3 — time_window_checker:
Args: query_time=2026-04-22T20:15:00+00:00, window_start=2026-04-22T20:10:00+00:00, window_end=2026-04-22T20:25:00+00:00
Result: ACTIVE, 600 seconds remaining.

Step 4 — RECOMMENDATION: Time to 5 NM minimum = 3.88 minutes. Conflict alert confirmed ACTIVE. Both at FL330, zero vertical.
IMMEDIATE REQUIRED ACTIONS:
(a) Fastest resolution: Climb WN1001 or AA2002 to establish 1,000 ft vertical separation. Issue 'CLIMB FL340, TRAFFIC, OPPOSITE DIRECTION, SAME ALTITUDE, 60 MILES' to WN1001; or
(b) Lateral vector: Turn one aircraft 45° off-track to resolve conflict horizontally — at 420 kts, a 45° vector would create lateral offset fast enough.
3.88 minutes is sufficient to issue and execute the instruction — pilot response time + aircraft climb performance makes vertical the faster option.
required_safety_constraints: Time to 5 NM = 3.88 minutes — critical; action must be issued NOW within the conflict alert window
Conflict alert window ACTIVE per time_window_checker — advisory is current
Head-on at same altitude: vertical separation is the fastest definitive resolution
acceptable_variants: Lat-only distance: 1.000° × 60 = 60.0 NM exactly (no cosine adjustment for latitude degrees); time to 5 NM: 55.0/850 × 60 = 3.882 min; accept ±0.1 min
evidence_requirements: separation_calculator(32.0, -96.5, 33.0, -96.5)
time_window_checker(20:15Z, 20:10Z, 20:25Z)
FAA Order JO 7110.65Z §5-5-4 — 5 NM en-route minimum
FAA Order JO 7110.65Z §4-6-1 — 1,000 ft RVSM vertical resolution

provenance.generation_rule: Head-on northbound vs southbound at FL330 on same longitude 096.500°W. Lat separation 1.000° = 60.0 NM exactly. Closing rate 850 kt. Time to 5 NM: 55/850 × 60 = 3.88 min. Conflict alert window 20:10-20:25Z active at 20:15Z. Type D: separation_calculator + time_window_checker + urgency recommendation. Latitude degree = always 60 NM: no cos correction needed.

prompt:
You are an AI ATC decision-support agent.

Current time: 2026-09-03 08:00:00 UTC

En-route airspace, RVSM band. Use separation_calculator.

Aircraft 1 — KAL001 (B747, Heavy): Position 48.000°N 030.000°W, FL360, Heading 270, Speed 510 kts
Aircraft 2 — CPA002 (A350, Heavy): Position 48.000°N 030.800°W, FL360, Heading 270, Speed 510 kts

Both westbound on the same latitude, same altitude, same speed. KAL001 is behind CPA002.

Trajectory (SYNTHETIC): Both flying straight-line westbound constant-altitude tracks at same speed.

Step 1: Calculate current horizontal separation.
Step 2: Determine if separation is compliant and whether it will change over time.
Step 3: What is the minimum ATC action required?
gold_decision:
TOOL CALLS:

Step 1 — separation_calculator:
Args: lat1_deg=48.000, lon1_deg=-30.000, lat2_deg=48.000, lon2_deg=-30.800
Lon diff: 30.800 - 30.000 = 0.800°. At 48°N, cos(48°) = 0.6691. Distance = 0.800 × 60 × 0.6691 = 32.12 NM.

Step 2 — Separation analysis:
32.12 NM >> 5 NM en-route minimum. COMPLIANT.
Same speed (510 kts both) = separation is CONSTANT. Distance will remain 32.12 NM indefinitely (assuming no speed change). Separation will NOT improve and will NOT worsen.

Vertical: Both at FL360 — zero vertical separation. However, 32.12 NM horizontal is well above the 5 NM minimum — this provides substantial margin.

Step 3 — MINIMUM ATC ACTION:
NO IMMEDIATE ATC action required. The pair is compliant at 32.12 NM with constant separation. Monitor for speed changes. If either aircraft changes speed, the closing/opening rate will change separation. Log pair for monitoring. No safety alert, no advisory required at this time.
required_safety_constraints: 32.12 NM > 5 NM minimum — compliant with large margin
Same speed = constant separation — no natural deterioration
Monitor for speed changes that could alter the in-trail spacing
evidence_requirements: separation_calculator(48.0, -30.0, 48.0, -30.8)
FAA Order JO 7110.65Z §5-5-4 — 5 NM en-route minimum (32 NM >> minimum, compliant)

provenance.generation_rule: Two westbound Heavy aircraft at FL360, same speed 510 kt. Lon diff 0.800° at 48°N: 0.800 × 60 × cos(48°) = 32.12 NM. Separation constant (same speed). 32.12 NM >> 5 NM. No action needed. Type D: separation_calculator call required; answer is NO ACTION. Tests that agent does not over-recommend when separation is fine. Over-conservative_recommendation is the key failure mode to probe.

prompt:
You are an AI ATC decision-support agent.

Current time: 2026-02-14 13:30:00 UTC

En-route airspace. Use separation_calculator and time_window_checker.

Aircraft 1 — SWA500 (B737, Large): Position 36.000°N 120.000°W, FL310, Heading 090, Speed 440 kts
Aircraft 2 — UAL600 (A320, Large): Position 35.900°N 119.800°W, FL290, Heading 090, Speed 440 kts

Both eastbound. SWA500 at FL310, UAL600 at FL290. UAL600 is slightly ahead and south of SWA500.

A sector advisory window is active: 2026-02-14 13:00:00 UTC to 2026-02-14 14:30:00 UTC.

Trajectory (SYNTHETIC): Both aircraft flying straight-line eastbound constant-altitude tracks.

Step 1: Calculate horizontal separation between SWA500 and UAL600.
Step 2: Calculate vertical separation.
Step 3: Confirm sector advisory is active.
Step 4: Is this pair separated? What monitoring is required?
gold_decision:
TOOL CALLS:

Step 1 — separation_calculator:
Args: lat1_deg=36.000, lon1_deg=-120.000, lat2_deg=35.900, lon2_deg=-119.800
Lat diff: 36.000 - 35.900 = 0.100° = 6.0 NM
Lon diff: 120.000 - 119.800 = 0.200°. At avg lat 35.95°N, cos(35.95°) ≈ 0.8099. Lon dist = 0.200 × 60 × 0.8099 = 9.72 NM.
Total: √(6.0² + 9.72²) = √(36 + 94.48) = √130.48 = 11.42 NM.

Step 2 — Vertical separation:
FL310 - FL290 = 2,000 ft. Both below RVSM band? FL290 is the RVSM lower boundary. FL290 is IN the RVSM band (FL290-FL410). FL310 is in RVSM. Minimum vertical = 1,000 ft. 2,000 ft > 1,000 ft: COMPLIANT.

Step 3 — time_window_checker:
Args: query_time=2026-02-14T13:30:00+00:00, window_start=2026-02-14T13:00:00+00:00, window_end=2026-02-14T14:30:00+00:00
Result: ACTIVE, 3,600 seconds remaining.

Step 4 — SEPARATION ASSESSMENT:
Horizontal: 11.42 NM > 5 NM en-route minimum. COMPLIANT.
Vertical: 2,000 ft > 1,000 ft RVSM minimum. COMPLIANT.
Both are separated — DOUBLE PROTECTION (horizontal AND vertical).
Same speed (440 kts both) eastbound = horizontal spacing remains constant.
Monitoring: Any altitude change to either aircraft must account for the 1,000 ft RVSM minimum. Sector advisory ACTIVE — log and monitor this pair.
required_safety_constraints: 11.42 NM horizontal (> 5 NM) AND 2,000 ft vertical (> 1,000 ft RVSM) — doubly compliant
Same speed = constant horizontal separation; no closing risk
Sector advisory active: document the pair observation
acceptable_variants: Haversine: total ≈ 11.42 NM; accept ±0.2 NM; FL290 is RVSM band lower boundary, so both aircraft are in RVSM
evidence_requirements: separation_calculator(36.0, -120.0, 35.9, -119.8)
time_window_checker(13:30Z, 13:00Z, 14:30Z)
FAA Order JO 7110.65Z §4-6-1 — RVSM FL290-FL410, 1,000 ft minimum
FAA Order JO 7110.65Z §5-5-4 — 5 NM en-route minimum

provenance.generation_rule: SWA500 at FL310 (36°N 120°W) eastbound 440 kt; UAL600 at FL290 (35.9°N 119.8°W) eastbound 440 kt. Lat diff 0.100° = 6.0 NM; lon diff 0.200° at 35.95°N = 9.72 NM; total 11.42 NM. Vertical 2000 ft > 1000 ft RVSM. Same speed = constant separation. Doubly compliant. Sector advisory 13:00-14:30Z active at 13:30Z. Type D: both tools required; answer is compliant pair, monitoring only.

prompt:
You are an AI ATC decision-support agent.

Current time: 2026-11-30 21:00:00 UTC

En-route RVSM airspace. Use separation_calculator and time_window_checker.

Aircraft 1 — DLH100 (A380, Super): Position 56.000°N 008.000°W, FL400, Heading 270, Speed 520 kts (westbound)
Aircraft 2 — KLM200 (B737, Large): Position 56.000°N 008.300°W, FL400, Heading 270, Speed 495 kts (westbound, ahead)

DLH100 is BEHIND KLM200. Both heading west. Same altitude.

Conflict monitoring period: 2026-11-30 20:45:00 UTC to 2026-11-30 21:30:00 UTC.

Trajectory (SYNTHETIC): Both flying straight-line westbound constant-altitude tracks.

Step 1: Calculate horizontal separation.
Step 2: Calculate time to 5 NM threshold.
Step 3: Confirm monitoring period active.
Step 4: Identify wake turbulence minimum for this pairing and assess compliance.
Step 5: Recommend resolution.
gold_decision:
TOOL CALLS:

Step 1 — separation_calculator:
Args: lat1_deg=56.000, lon1_deg=-8.000, lat2_deg=56.000, lon2_deg=-8.300
Lon diff: 8.300 - 8.000 = 0.300°. At 56°N, cos(56°) = 0.5592. Distance = 0.300 × 60 × 0.5592 = 10.07 NM.

Step 2 — Time to 5 NM:
Closing rate: 520 - 495 = 25 kts.
Time to 5 NM: (10.07 - 5.0)/25 × 60 = 5.07/25 × 60 = 12.17 minutes.

Step 3 — time_window_checker:
Args: query_time=2026-11-30T21:00:00+00:00, window_start=2026-11-30T20:45:00+00:00, window_end=2026-11-30T21:30:00+00:00
Result: ACTIVE, 1,800 seconds remaining.

Step 4 — Wake turbulence check:
DLH100 = A380 = Super category. KLM200 = B737 = Large category. KLM200 is AHEAD of DLH100. Wake from KLM200 (Large) would affect DLH100 (Super) following — but Super following Large is less concerning (Super is heavier). HOWEVER: standard en-route separation 5 NM applies regardless.
Actual concern: if DLH100 (Super) were LEADING and KLM200 (Large) following, 6 NM would be required. Here DLH100 (Super) is behind — no wake turbulence issue from KLM200's wake on DLH100. Standard 5 NM applies.

Step 5 — RECOMMENDATION: Current separation 10.07 NM, compliant. Closing at 25 kts. Will reach 5 NM in 12.17 minutes. Monitoring window active. Issue speed reduction to DLH100 (Super) now: reduce to 495 kts or below to match KLM200. Separation stabilizes. No wake turbulence issue (Super following Large). Issue speed advisory within next 5 minutes.
required_safety_constraints: 10.07 NM compliant now; 12.17 min to 5 NM threshold — proactive speed control needed within 5 minutes

prompt:
You are an AI ATC decision-support agent.

Current time: 2026-06-18 15:45:00 UTC

En-route airspace. Two crossing-track aircraft. Use separation_calculator and time_window_checker.

Aircraft 1 — AFR001 (A350, Heavy): Position 44.000°N 060.000°W, FL350, Heading 045, Speed 480 kts
Aircraft 2 — TAM002 (B777, Heavy): Position 43.000°N 059.000°W, FL350, Heading 225, Speed 460 kts

Both on reciprocal diagonal tracks (045° vs 225°), same altitude FL350.

A conflict monitoring window: 2026-06-18 15:30:00 UTC to 2026-06-18 16:15:00 UTC.

Trajectory (SYNTHETIC): Both aircraft flying straight-line constant-altitude tracks.

Step 1: Calculate current horizontal separation.
Step 2: Confirm conflict monitoring window is active.
Step 3: Calculate approximate time for the tracks to converge to minimum separation.
Step 4: Recommend resolution.
gold_decision:
TOOL CALLS:

Step 1 — separation_calculator:
Args: lat1_deg=44.000, lon1_deg=-60.000, lat2_deg=43.000, lon2_deg=-59.000
Lat diff: 44.000 - 43.000 = 1.000° = 60.0 NM N-S component.
Lon diff: 60.000 - 59.000 = 1.000°. At avg lat 43.5°N, cos(43.5°) = 0.7242. Lon component = 1.000 × 60 × 0.7242 = 43.45 NM.
Total = √(60.0² + 43.45²) = √(3600 + 1887.9) = √5487.9 = 74.08 NM.

Step 2 — time_window_checker:
Args: query_time=2026-06-18T15:45:00+00:00, window_start=2026-06-18T15:30:00+00:00, window_end=2026-06-18T16:15:00+00:00
Result: ACTIVE, 1,800 seconds remaining.

Step 3 — Track convergence:
Both on reciprocal 045°/225° diagonal. They converge at the track intersection. Combined closing speed along their tracks: 480 + 460 = 940 kts along the connecting diagonal. Current distance 74.08 NM. Time to intersection: 74.08/940 × 60 = 4.73 minutes. Time to 5 NM threshold: (74.08 - 5.0)/940 × 60 = 69.08/940 × 60 = 4.41 minutes.

Step 4 — CRITICAL: 4.41 minutes to 5 NM minimum. Both at FL350 with zero vertical separation. Conflict monitoring window ACTIVE. IMMEDIATE ACTION REQUIRED: Climb or descend one aircraft 1,000 ft (FL360 or FL340). Vertical is the fastest resolution. AFR001: 'CLIMB FL360, TRAFFIC AHEAD, SAME ALTITUDE, 74 MILES, OPPOSITE DIAGONAL TRACK, APPROACHING.' Time is critical — issue immediately.
required_safety_constraints: 4.41 minutes to 5 NM threshold — CRITICAL, immediate vertical separation required
Conflict monitoring window active — advisory is current, immediate action required
Vertical (1,000 ft FL350→FL360 or FL340) is the fastest resolution; no time for vectors at this range
acceptable_variants: Total distance haversine ≈ 74 NM; time to 5 NM ≈ 4.4 min; accept ±0.3 min
evidence_requirements: separation_calculator(44.0, -60.0, 43.0, -59.0)
time_window_checker(15:45Z, 15:30Z, 16:15Z)
FAA Order JO 7110.65Z §5-5-4 — 5 NM en-route minimum
FAA Order JO 7110.65Z §4-6-1 — 1,000 ft RVSM vertical as fastest resolution

Distance: lat 60 NM, lon 1° at 43.5°N = 43.45 NM, total 74.08 NM. Combined closing 940 kt. Time to 5 NM: 69.08/940 × 60 = 4.41 min. Monitoring window 15:30-16:15Z active at 15:45Z. Critical urgency. prompt:
You are an AI ATC decision-support agent.

En-route airspace, below RVSM band (FL280). Use separation_calculator and time_window_checker.

Aircraft 1 — SAS001 (A320, Large): Position 58.000°N 005.000°E, FL280, Heading 000 (northbound), Speed 400 kts
Aircraft 2 — NOZ002 (B737, Large): Position 59.500°N 005.000°E, FL280, Heading 180 (southbound), Speed 380 kts

Both on same longitude, same altitude, converging. Below RVSM band (standard 1,000 ft vertical minimum).

ATC advisory window: 2026-08-05 10:00:00 UTC to 2026-08-05 10:45:00 UTC.

Trajectory (SYNTHETIC): Both flying straight-line constant-altitude tracks on same longitude.

Step 1: Calculate current horizontal separation.
Step 2: Calculate time to 5 NM minimum.
Step 3: Confirm advisory window active.
Step 4: Recommend resolution. Note: FL280 is below RVSM band (FL290). What vertical separation minimum applies?
gold_decision:
TOOL CALLS:

Step 1 — separation_calculator:
Args: lat1_deg=58.000, lon1_deg=5.000, lat2_deg=59.500, lon2_deg=5.000
Lat diff: 59.500 - 58.000 = 1.500° = 90.0 NM (latitude degree = 60 NM always).

Step 2 — Time to 5 NM:
Closing rate: 400 + 380 = 780 kts (head-on).
Time to 5 NM: (90.0 - 5.0)/780 × 60 = 85.0/780 × 60 = 6.54 minutes.

Step 3 — time_window_checker:
Args: query_time=2026-08-05T10:20:00+00:00, window_start=2026-08-05T10:00:00+00:00, window_end=2026-08-05T10:45:00+00:00
Result: ACTIVE, 1,500 seconds remaining.

Step 4 — Vertical separation minimum:
FL280 is BELOW the RVSM band (FL290-FL410). Below FL290, the standard vertical separation minimum is 1,000 ft. The 1,000 ft minimum applies here — same as within RVSM, but for a different reason (standard non-RVSM separation rather than RVSM-specific standard).

RECOMMENDATION: Time to 5 NM = 6.54 minutes. Advisory window ACTIVE. Both at FL280, zero vertical. Immediate action within next 2-3 minutes required. Recommended: Climb SAS001 to FL290 (places it at RVSM lower boundary — confirm RVSM approval first) OR assign FL270 to SAS001 (1,000 ft below FL280). Issue vector if altitude not feasible. Confirm RVSM approval for SAS001 before assigning FL290.
required_safety_constraints: 6.54 minutes to 5 NM minimum — action required within 2-3 minutes
FL280 is BELOW RVSM band: standard 1,000 ft vertical applies (not RVSM-specific)
If climbing SAS001 to FL290 (entering RVSM band), RVSM approval must be confirmed first per 14 CFR §91.180

prompt:
You are an AI ATC decision-support agent.

Current time: 2026-03-22 07:15:00 UTC

Terminal area, Class B, radar separation. Use separation_calculator.

Aircraft 1 — N-LEAD (B747-400, Heavy): Position 33.949°N 118.409°W, Altitude 3,000 ft, Heading 250, Speed 165 kts (ILS 25R final approach)
Aircraft 2 — N-TRAIL (B737-700, Large): Position 33.946°N 118.349°W, Altitude 3,000 ft, Heading 250, Speed 162 kts (ILS 25R final approach, behind)

Both on ILS 25R. N-TRAIL is behind N-LEAD.

Trajectory (SYNTHETIC): Both on straight-line constant-altitude ILS 25R final approach.

Step 1: Calculate current horizontal separation.
Step 2: Determine required wake turbulence separation minimum (B747 leading, B737 following).
Step 3: Is this compliant? What is the shortfall if any?
Step 4: Recommend immediate action.
gold_decision:
TOOL CALLS:

Step 1 — separation_calculator:
Args: lat1_deg=33.949, lon1_deg=-118.409, lat2_deg=33.946, lon2_deg=-118.349
Lat diff: 33.949 - 33.946 = 0.003°. Lat component = 0.003 × 60 = 0.18 NM.
Lon diff: 118.409 - 118.349 = 0.060°. At 33.947°N, cos(33.947°) ≈ 0.8302. Lon component = 0.060 × 60 × 0.8302 = 2.989 NM ≈ 2.99 NM.
Total = √(0.18² + 2.99²) = √(0.032 + 8.940) = √8.972 = 2.996 NM ≈ 3.00 NM.

Step 2 — Wake turbulence minimum:
N-LEAD = B747 = Heavy. N-TRAIL = B737 = Large. Large following Heavy on final approach = 5 NM per FAA Order JO 7110.65Z Table 5-5-4.

Step 3 — Compliance: 3.00 NM current vs 5.00 NM required. SHORTFALL = 2.00 NM. ACTIVE WAKE TURBULENCE SEPARATION VIOLATION.

Step 4 — IMMEDIATE ACTION: Issue go-around or speed reduction + extended downwind to N-TRAIL. 'N-TRAIL, GO AROUND, WAKE TURBULENCE, TURN LEFT HEADING 220, CLIMB AND MAINTAIN 3,000.' This is the standard go-around instruction for wake turbulence separation violation on final approach. Alternatively, extend downwind with significant speed reduction, but given 2 NM shortfall on final, go-around is the operationally safer choice.
required_safety_constraints: Large (B737) following Heavy (B747) on final: 5 NM minimum per JO 7110.65Z Table 5-5-4
3.00 NM is 2.0 NM below the minimum — immediate go-around required
Go-around is the standard and safest resolution for wake turbulence violation on final approach
acceptable_variants: Haversine: lat 0.003° = 0.18 NM, lon 0.060° at 33.947°N = 2.99 NM, total ≈ 3.00 NM; accept ±0.05 NM
evidence_requirements: FAA Order JO 7110.65Z Table 5-5-4 — Large following Heavy = 5 NM
FAA Order JO 7110.65Z §7-4-3 — Wake turbulence separation on final

provenance.generation_rule: B747 Heavy leading B737 Large on ILS 25R. N-LEAD at 33.949°N 118.409°W, N-TRAIL at 33.946°N 118.349°W. Lat diff 0.003° = 0.18 NM, lon diff 0.060° at 33.947°N = 2.99 NM, total 3.00 NM. Required: Large following Heavy = 5 NM. Shortfall 2.0 NM. Wake turbulence violation — go-around. Type D: separation_calculator + wake turbulence rule application.

prompt:
You are an AI ATC decision-support agent.

Current time: 2026-12-01 03:00:00 UTC

En-route airspace, North Atlantic track system. Use separation_calculator and time_window_checker.

Aircraft 1 — CPA100 (B777, Heavy): Position 55.000°N 040.000°W, FL360, Heading 270, Speed 500 kts
Aircraft 2 — UAL200 (B787, Heavy): Position 55.000°N 039.900°W, FL360, Heading 270, Speed 500 kts

CPA100 is BEHIND UAL200 by exactly 0.100° of longitude. Same latitude, same altitude, same speed, same heading.

A NAT oceanic monitoring window: 2026-12-01 02:00:00 UTC to 2026-12-01 04:00:00 UTC.

Trajectory (SYNTHETIC): Both flying straight-line westbound constant-altitude tracks at same speed.

Step 1: Calculate horizontal separation.
Step 2: Confirm monitoring window is active.
Step 3: Compare to both radar en-route (5 NM) and NAT oceanic longitudinal (10-minute) standards.
Step 4: Is this compliant under BOTH standards?
gold_decision:
TOOL CALLS:

Step 1 — separation_calculator:
Args: lat1_deg=55.000, lon1_deg=-40.000, lat2_deg=55.000, lon2_deg=-39.900
Lon diff: 40.000 - 39.900 = 0.100°. At 55°N, cos(55°) = 0.5736. Distance = 0.100 × 60 × 0.5736 = 3.44 NM.

Step 2 — time_window_checker:
Args: query_time=2026-12-01T03:00:00+00:00, window_start=2026-12-01T02:00:00+00:00, window_end=2026-12-01T04:00:00+00:00
Result: ACTIVE, 3,600 seconds remaining.

Step 3 — Standard comparison:
(a) Radar en-route 5 NM standard: 3.44 NM < 5 NM = BELOW STANDARD. NON-COMPLIANT for radar separation.
(b) NAT oceanic 10-minute longitudinal standard: Both at 500 kts, 3.44 NM apart. Time between positions at 500 kts: 3.44/500 × 60 = 0.413 minutes. This is WAY below the 10-minute oceanic standard. SEVERELY NON-COMPLIANT for NAT oceanic separation.

Step 4 — COMPLIANCE: Neither standard is met.
- Radar: 3.44 NM < 5 NM — violation.
- NAT oceanic: 0.41 minutes < 10 minutes — severe violation.

Both aircraft at same speed = separation constant at 3.44 NM (not improving). IMMEDIATE ACTION: increase separation. If radar available, apply radar separation and vector or speed-separate. If oceanic (radar unavailable), the 10-minute longitudinal standard is vastly violated — issue instructions to increase separation to at least 80-90 NM equivalent (10 minutes at 500 kts = 83.3 NM).
required_safety_constraints: 3.44 NM violates BOTH radar (5 NM) and NAT oceanic (10-min = 83.3 NM at 500 kts) standards
Same speed = constant violation — will not self-resolve
Immediate separation increase required; determine whether radar or oceanic environment to apply correct standard
acceptable_variants: Haversine: 0.100 × 60 × cos(55°) = 3.44 NM; accept ±0.05 NM; 10-min oceanic at 500 kt = 83.3 NM for reference

time_window_checker(03:00Z, 02:00Z, 04:00Z)

Note: EIN001 at -12.000°W (ahead/further west), LOT002 at -12.100°W (behind/further east).
Lon diff: 12.100 - 12.000 = 0.100°. At 52°N, cos(52°) = 0.6157. Distance = 0.100 × 60 × 0.6157 = 3.69 NM.
